preview_text:
The model favors England at 63%, giving Panama a 12% chance of victory. England enters as the clear favorite, though the model assigns Panama roughly one-in-eight odds—a non-trivial upset probability.

The forecast distributes probability across all three outcomes, with draws at 25% suggesting meaningful competitive balance in the match itself. England's 39-point edge over Panama in win probability indicates the model sees a likely England victory, but the substantial draw probability and Panama's double-digit upset chance imply the gap in underlying strength is moderate rather than vast.

The sportsbook consensus shifts weight decisively toward England, pricing them at 77% and compressing Panama to 9% and draws to 14%. The market is 13 percentage points more bullish on England than the model and roughly half as confident in a draw. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment of match uncertainty is correct.
